sentences.text: Kelly was just in the waiting room.
Ok, Kelly is back in the waiting room.
I'm gonna let Kelly in step by work.
Kelly.
Hey, how is everybody doing?
Doing well?
Yourself?
Good, thanks.
Good.
How was your weekend?
Uh, it was all right.
It was not as long as it needs to be like, every weekend, I think, but it was pretty good.
Kelly, did I see you out, um, doing the bar crawl for Saint Patty's Day.
We, I was actually in Panama City.
We actually had a friend of ours who passed away like a month ago and we got a bunch of people together to do an Irish wake for him.
So he was military.
So people flew in from all over the country and it was a lot more drinking than I would have normally done for Saint Patrick's Day.
But, uh, it ended up being really, it ended up being really good.
Yeah.
Well, you look like you're in pretty good shape.
To be honest.
I, I feel better than I thought I was going to yesterday afternoon.
So
you and, uh, about half the US population, I think.
So.
How about you guys?
Anybody of you go out and bend, bend an elbow for Saint Patrick a
little bit.
I hosted a, hosted a, a brunch here.
My uh brother is local so he brought his, uh, his niece over.
So it was, it was classy for a little bit and they departed and we went, we did go downtown for an hour.
So excellent.
Yeah,
I was gonna say, I mean, Schneck, you're in Chicago.
I feel like it's, it's like the ultimate weekend in Chicago.
It's the ultimate weekend you want to avoid going downtown in Chicago.
I
love watching them die the river every year when they have the boats go out.
It's so much fun to see that.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Um I did, I did go down for that.
I did take a jog in the morning and just kind of walk over and watch that.
It was pretty cool.
Very good.
And I am the only one from Courtland you'll have on the phone today.
Carrie is not able to make it.
She's prepping.
We have an all day or an all week operations meeting here in Atlanta that starts tomorrow morning.
So she's prepping for that.
And then Cassie will jump on on the call where we actually discuss contracts and things she'll get in once we get into the nitty gritty.
So I told Carrie I'd just connect with you guys today to understand kind of what the new offerings are.
What else we may want to add so that she can incorporate that into um the contract discussion.
OK.
Sounds good, Kelly.
And then uh noticed Tim was on the invite as well.

And so I know we touched on that last week that there's this new uh operational revenue team and they just kind of kick off by understanding uh I guess kind of the objectives of, of that team and uh some of the primary issues or, or problems that we're looking to solve uh this year that maybe a apartment I can speak to while we start to get into product.
Ok.
And I apologize, I didn't even see him on the invite if he was available.
I don't know if he responded either way, but I know that he, we just did a live town hall that he was a part of.
So it's been kind of busy over here.
Um But I can share some of that with you and I'm happy to share with you kind of what his like his vision statement and everything is now being circulated around.
So essentially our focuses are on maintaining our top line revenue income.
Um And part of that is going to be the the expense side of things, right?
So it's kind of that focus on top line with that typical balance of rent and occupancy that we, you know, manage on a daily basis in revenue management.
And then we're also trying to incorporate more of our rentable items and our other ancillary income streams, fees and things like that outside of rental revenue, especially in this down market.
Obviously, we're looking to make sure we're maximizing every line.
So we think there's some opportunity on those other and income lines to focus on.
Um And then when it comes to the cost balance, we're really heavily leading into our retention.
So we're looking into ways that we can maximize our renewal process looking at that net revenue.
Um what with all of the costs considered?

Um So really looking at that, what we're considering like a refill or replacement cost for the customers, including vacancy and everything else that comes with it um to make sure that we are going out with a renewal strategy that benefits the business as well as the residents in today's market.
So those are probably the the three big areas that we're focusing, so the balance, the other ancillary income and then the retention.
Great.
And I know we also talked briefly about unifying the comps that you guys are all using to benchmark against the market.
Understand what that looks like.
That's still something that I think maybe we said the next 4 to 6 weeks, it's gonna be kind of a cross departmental effort remembering that, right.
Yeah, and that's kind of just a supporting factor of all of those issues, right?

Like anywhere where we're benchmarking or we're comparing, especially when it comes to how we're pricing the deals we know that locking in and looking at and considering the right comps is, is key for us.
Um So that is one initiative that falls under that, that larger umbrella.
Yeah, got it.
And in terms of how these, these company wide or, or, or revenue specific initiatives flow into the data that you all would be utilizing or how you'd be looking at the data.
Is there anything in particular you're gonna look at differently or you'd like to look at this coming year, given those priorities?
Um As far as how we're using the A IQ data, I think the comps is the biggest piece of it, right?
I think we do have the right data in house.
We've, and we have a kind of a an infrastructure that we've built out based on the feed data that we're getting.
So we're locking into the right view right now.
The challenge is that everyone is self selecting that competitive set and everybody's doing it a little bit differently.
So I think locking that down on our end.
So it has less to do with the incoming data and more of how we're utilizing it.
Got it and, and in terms of our role in helping you all do that, how could we both be most helpful?
Um I think the historic data is one of the biggest things that we have right now.

We're really trying to get more of that the, the competitive pricing on the historical view so that we can understand where our market position has shifted versus those specific comps over time, even without the comp defined.
I think that's helpful even with the broader data set to get that historic.
So I think that's one of the big ones on our end that we're looking for a IQ to support.
Um And the other we are already doing, which is making strides on getting us more of the specials information
can speak to that.
Kelly
one question for you on the historic data having that going back to June 2020 what would that um how would that help you make different decisions?
Or what type of decisions would you hope to make that?
Um You couldn't previously as it relates to your market position.
I
think it's a great question.

I think it really is kind of that trend to understand if we are, if we have a value add community, obviously a big part of our portfolio is that value add, if we can see the, the the true rental change, so that we can kind of help to understand how much of our lift was based on market changes, how much of it was based on that ro why or that kind of value add strategy is one of the keys.

Um And I think the other is just making sure that we're we're keeping up with the market, right, that we're not missing out on opportunities for growth because of what's going on in our walls and that we're keeping that broader view of what's going on with us versus um in a, in a future state where we don't have that transactional data to look at, you know, like how we're going to make sure that we're maintaining that because as, you know, being a real paid shop, that's part of what we're doing right now is really kind of understanding us versus competitors.
Um And in a future state where we're going to potentially lose that transactional data, we think A IQ is the best supplement for that.
And obviously, it's so important to understand where the relationship is between those comps that we're making the gains to our aspirational set as we're going through.

And so again, over time moving forward, obviously, that's helpful, but just having a starting point to look back and say, OK, this is where we are, this is where we've been, are we set up for success or do we, you know, we need to change our strategy?
So, and it really is, you say in June 2020 I think that's great.
I think we in revenue management.
I'm always looking at a kind of a T 24.
Um So I really like to see two years worth of growth so that we can understand the seasonality and those patterns within the
markets.
And I, sorry, one last question, um you, you mentioned the, the um the different comp sets by the different teams.
So, just to be, I would just wanted to put a finer point on that.
Are there how I think we've mentioned in the past four or maybe, uh, different teams that use different comp sets?
Could you just say a little share a little bit more about the different teams and the way they're looking at the comps differently?
Absolutely.
Um, so I would think there's a, there's definitely a lot of overlap with the things that all four of us are considering.
Um marketing, I would say tends to lean more toward product comparability than anything having to do with pricing or hard data or whatever.
They, they think of things more of a an opportunity.
So they're looking at more of a product comparison.
Um and then revenue management, obviously, we're all about rate and distance.
I know those are two primaries on you.
Um Investments is one of the thirds.
So investments is going to look at things very similarly to us, but we'll have more of an aspirational vision of comp sets, especially for those value add deals when we initially do the underwriting.
And then the fourth is operations and we find that that's where we have the most, I would say the widest variety in, in approaches, right?
We have, you know, 200 plus community managers out there, all who have been raised through their career with different things in mind.
So I would say every individual within our operations team has kind of a varied view.

Um, and theirs is gonna come down a lot, probably a revenue management marketing mix where they're looking at product as well as, you know, the people who are walking in the door, what are the comps that they're saying that they're going to shop?
Right.

So, it's a little bit more, um, maybe there's more flux in what they think their comps are from one day to the other out of all of the different regions because they're getting that feedback from the market.
Thank
you.
Mhm Absolutely.

To your team.

Those are the things that we're really focusing on.

Telling you differs between the market survey that they're doing via phone and the information that we're providing through a IQ, you know, where is there a mismatch?

Um So all of that helpful information just to help us understand where there's significant velocity difference that the data doesn't support.
Thank you.
